behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources." Slot B (decisive boundary): handoff across collector modules -> aggregation -> summary-input builder. Slot C (miss if lowered to Unit): a field-mapping or merge bug between modules can drop one source even though each function unit test passes. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because this behavior can be reproduced with in-process fakes; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because it checks payload composition, not model judgment or multi-step agent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real aggregation/report modules together with three fake source adapters returning distinct sentinel values; assert the exact summary-input payload contains all three sections and expected keys after cross-module handoff.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "when one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note ... rather than aborting." Slot B (decisive boundary): handoff/error-propagation across source client, report assembler, and section renderer. Slot C (miss if lowered to Unit): one module may catch the exception but another module still treats missing data as fatal, causing pipeline abort. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because a simulated adapter failure exercises the same control-flow boundary; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because correctness here is fallback orchestration, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run report assembly with one source adapter raising a timeout/error and two adapters succeeding; verify process completes, failed section contains `data unavailable`, and other sections still render.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data." Slot B (decisive boundary): model-output correctness (semantic faithfulness of generated narrative). Slot C (miss if lowered to System): deterministic fixtures/stubs can pass while the real model hallucinates, omits, or misstates key values. Rejection gate: Workflow is disqualified because behavior text specifies a single summary-generation judgment, not multiple sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use fixed input datasets with known key values and run the real summary-generation model; evaluate factual coverage/consistency criteria (e.g., required values mentioned and numerically correct) across repeated runs with acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly." Slot B (decisive boundary): real infrastructure (HTTP/API connectivity + SMTP/mail delivery + inbox retrieval). Slot C (miss if lowered to Integration): mocked mail transport can hide MIME/auth/TLS/routing issues where app 'sends' successfully but inbox never receives. Rejection gate: Agentic is disqualified by behavior text because success criterion is end-to-end delivery to inbox, not quality of model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the script against reachable test instances/endpoints and a real test mailbox; assert one delivered message appears for configured recipients with expected subject/body/attachments and no fatal pipeline errors.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "email delivery service is unreachable ... saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run." Slot B (decisive boundary): real infrastructure (network failure semantics + filesystem persistence across runs + scheduler-triggered second run behavior). Slot C (miss if lowered to Integration): in-process mocks may not reveal real write-path/permission issues or retry behavior under actual connection failures between runs. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because this behavior is transport-failure recovery, not model-output correctness or multi-step agent reasoning.
behaviors.test_description: Run 1: execute script with mail endpoint intentionally unreachable; verify fallback file is created with report payload. Run 2 (next scheduled invocation in test harness): restore mail service and verify queued report is retried and delivered, with fallback state cleared/updated appropriately.
